dialogue.line: Stewie, what's this 'caching' thing? Is it where you hide cash?
No, you simpleton. Imagine caching as your brain storing quick notes!
Notes in the brain? Sounds handy!
Exactly! Caching stores frequently accessed data for fast retrieval.
Computers use it to save time reloading the same stuff.
It speeds up processes by keeping data ready on stand-by.
Kinda like how I keep snacks handy during TV time?
Precisely! Websites load faster thanks to caching, like instant macaroni!
So it's like instant brain snacks for computers?
Well put, Peter. Now if only you could cache some intelligence!
